content.text:
Build a Cal AI Clone — AI Calorie Tracker
Snap a photo of any meal and instantly get calorie counts, macros, and nutritional breakdowns powered by AI.

CHALLENGE BRIEF
Copy
Reference app: Cal AI (Calorie Tracker) — one of the highest-revenue AI health apps on the App Store.

What to build: A mobile app where users photograph their food and receive an instant AI-powered nutritional breakdown — calories, protein, carbs, fat. Include a daily food diary, macro progress rings, and streak tracking.

Key features:

Camera/photo upload → AI food recognition → macro breakdown
Daily diary with running calorie total
Macro progress rings (protein / carbs / fat)
Weekly history charts
Streak and goal tracking
Reference apps to study: Cal AI, MenuFit, MeAgain, Oasis

Scoring focus: AI integration quality, diary UX, and data visualization polish.

RESOURCES
View reference app
Download starter template
⭐
Get Priority Review
Using our starter template? Please star the repository on GitHub! We actively prioritize grading submissions from developers who support the open-source templates.

HOW YOU'LL BE JUDGED
Reviewed in this order — nail the top items first.

1
Screenshots

First impression of your UI — clear, polished screens of all major flows.

2
Loom walkthrough

A quick intro and full app demo showing it working end-to-end.

3
AI logs

Your AI-assisted development process — quality of prompts and iterations.

4
Code quality

Clean architecture, sensible structure, readable code.

5
Reflection

Honest write-up of what was easy, difficult, and what you'd change.

PRIZES
1st place
$50
2nd place
$40
3rd place
$30
4th place
$20
5th place
$10
Top 3 finalists may also be considered for a role at 8x Social.

input.content:
# Build a Cal AI Clone — AI Calorie Tracker

## Context
The goal is a polished mobile app (run as a web app for demo/screenshots) where a user
photographs a meal and instantly gets an AI nutritional breakdown — calories + protein/carbs/fat —
plus a daily food diary, macro progress rings, weekly history charts, and streak/goal tracking.
This is a 3-day challenge judged primarily on **screenshots → Loom demo → AI logs → code quality → reflection**,
so the bar is: visually striking, runs end-to-end with real AI, clean architecture.

The directory is empty (no starter template). We build fresh.

**Locked decisions (from the user):**
- Stack: React Native + **Expo (Expo Router)**, run target = **web browser** (`expo start --web`).
- AI: **Google Gemini** (free key from Google AI Studio, `gemini-2.0-flash`), structured JSON output,
  with a **mock fallback** so the app fully works before a key is added.
- Streak rule: a day counts if the user **logs ≥1 meal**.
- Goals: **sensible defaults + editable Profile** (no full onboarding flow).
- Camera on web = **file upload / image picker**.

## Tech stack & dependencies
- `expo` (latest SDK via `create-expo-app`), `expo-router` — file-based navigation, web support.
- `react-native-svg` — macro rings + weekly bar chart (no heavy chart lib needed).
- `zustand` + `@react-native-async-storage/async-storage` (persist middleware) — state + local persistence (uses `localStorage` on web).
- `expo-image-picker` — pick/capture photo, returns base64 (works on web as file picker).
- `expo-linear-gradient`, `expo-haptics` (no-op on web) — polish.
- `date-fns` — date/streak helpers.
- TypeScript throughout.

## Project structure
```
ai-calorie-tracker/
  app/
    _layout.tsx                # root stack, theme/StatusBar, store hydration gate
    (tabs)/
      _layout.tsx              # tab bar: Diary · Insights · (center) Add · Profile
      index.tsx                # DIARY: calorie ring + macro rings + today's meals + running total
      insights.tsx             # weekly bar chart, 7-day avg, streak badge
      profile.tsx              # editable goals (cal/P/C/F), reset data
    add.tsx                    # modal: pick image → analyze (AI) → editable result → save
  src/
    components/
      CalorieRing.tsx          # big SVG ring: consumed vs goal, "remaining" center label
      MacroRing.tsx            # small SVG ring per macro (protein/carbs/fat) w/ color
      MacroRings.tsx           # row of 3 MacroRing
      MealCard.tsx             # thumbnail, name, kcal, macro chips, swipe/long-press delete
      WeeklyBarChart.tsx       # 7-day SVG bars vs goal line
      StreakBadge.tsx          # flame + count
      EmptyState.tsx
      Screen.tsx               # safe-area + bg wrapper
    services/
      ai.ts                    # analyzeFoodImage(base64) -> Nutrition  (chooses gemini | mock)
      gemini.ts                # Gemini REST call w/ responseSchema (structured JSON)
      mockAnalyzer.ts          # realistic randomized fallback (named foods + plausible macros)
    store/
      useDiaryStore.ts         # zustand: entries[], goals, add/remove/update, selectors
    lib/
      nutrition.ts             # dayTotals(), remaining(), macroCalories()
      streak.ts                # currentStreak(entries)
      date.ts                  # todayISO(), last7Days(), formatting
    theme/
      colors.ts, typography.ts # dark, vibrant palette; macro colors
  assets/                      # icon, splash, sample food images for demo
  .env.example                 # EXPO_PUBLIC_GEMINI_API_KEY=
  app.json
```

## Data model (TypeScript)
```ts
type Nutrition = { name: string; servingDesc: string;
                   calories: number; protein: number; carbs: number; fat: number;
                   confidence?: number };
type FoodEntry = Nutrition & { id: string; dateISO: string; createdAt: number;
                               imageUri?: string; source: 'ai' | 'manual' };
type Goals = { calories: number; protein: number; carbs: number; fat: number };
// store: { entries: FoodEntry[]; goals: Goals (default 2000 / 150P / 200C / 65F) }
```

## Implementation steps
1. **Scaffold**: `npx create-expo-app@latest ai-calorie-tracker -t` (Expo Router + TS template) inside the working dir; install deps above; add `.env.example` and `theme/`.
2. **Store** (`useDiaryStore.ts`): zustand + persist(AsyncStorage). Actions: `addEntry`, `removeEntry`, `updateEntry`, `setGoals`. Selectors: `entriesForDate`, `dayTotals`. Add a hydration flag so the root layout can gate render until storage loads.
3. **AI service**:
   - `gemini.ts`: POST to `generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/models/gemini-2.0-flash:generateContent?key=…` with the base64 image part + a prompt; use `responseMimeType: application/json` + `responseSchema` to force the `Nutrition` shape. Parse + clamp/sanitize numbers.
   - `ai.ts`: if `EXPO_PUBLIC_GEMINI_API_KEY` present → gemini, else → `mockAnalyzer` (and fall back to mock on any network/parse error so the demo never breaks). Note in code the key-exposure tradeoff (client-side key is fine for a demo; a thin proxy would be the production fix).
4. **Add flow** (`add.tsx`): `expo-image-picker` → show image → "Analyzing…" state → call `ai.analyzeFoodImage` → editable form (name, serving, kcal, P/C/F sliders/inputs, confidence pill) → Save → `addEntry` → route back to Diary. Manual-add path (skip photo) for completeness.
5. **Diary** (`index.tsx`): header date; `CalorieRing` (consumed/goal, remaining in center); `MacroRings` row; running total; list of today's `MealCard`s with delete; FAB/empty state linking to Add.
6. **Insights** (`insights.tsx`): `WeeklyBarChart` (last 7 days calories with goal line), 7-day average, `StreakBadge` via `lib/streak.ts` (consecutive days ending today with ≥1 entry).
7. **Profile** (`profile.tsx`): edit goal numbers (with live macro-calorie sanity), reset data, small about/AI-status line (shows whether real AI or mock is active).
8. **Polish**: consistent dark theme, rounded cards, gradient accents, ring fill animation (Animated/Reanimated), haptics on save, seed a few sample entries on first run so screenshots look full, app icon/splash.

## Key reuse / conventions
- One SVG ring primitive (`MacroRing`) parameterized by color/size; `CalorieRing` reuses the same arc math from `lib/nutrition.ts`.
- All totals/streak logic lives in `lib/` (pure functions) — easy to read and unit-friendly.
- Single source of AI truth in `services/ai.ts`; screens never call Gemini directly.
- Colors centralized in `theme/colors.ts` (protein/carbs/fat each a fixed hue used everywhere).

## Verification (end-to-end)
1. `cd ai-calorie-tracker && npx expo start --web` → opens in browser.
2. Without a key: Add → pick a food image → mock analysis returns plausible macros → save → Diary rings + running total update; add 2–3 → list grows.
3. Insights tab: weekly bars render, streak shows ≥1; Profile: change calorie goal → ring re-scales.
4. With a key: put `EXPO_PUBLIC_GEMINI_API_KEY=…` in `.env`, restart, upload a real food photo → verify Gemini returns real recognition + macros; kill network → confirm graceful mock fallback.
5. Sanity: delete an entry → totals decrease; reload page → data persists (AsyncStorage/localStorage).

## Deliverables for the challenge (after build)
- Screenshots of all flows (Diary/rings, Add/AI result, Insights/charts, Profile).
- README with setup + how to get a free Gemini key + the AI-integration writeup.
- (User records the Loom + reflection.)

## Notes / tradeoffs
- Client-side Gemini key is acceptable for this demo; production would proxy via a server.
- Web target means `expo-camera` live capture is replaced by image upload — intentional for screenshot quality. The same code runs on a device via Expo Go if desired.

# Build a Cal AI Clone — AI Calorie Tracker

The goal is a polished mobile app (run as a web app for demo/screenshots) where a user
photographs a meal and instantly gets an AI nutritional breakdown — calories + protein/carbs/fat —
plus a daily food diary, macro progress rings, weekly history charts, and streak/goal tracking.
This is a 3-day challenge judged primarily on **screenshots → Loom demo → AI logs → code quality → reflection**,
so the bar is: visually striking, runs end-to-end with real AI, clean architecture.

The directory is empty (no starter template). We build fresh.

**Locked decisions (from the user):**
- Stack: React Native + **Expo (Expo Router)**, run target = **web browser** (`expo start --web`).
- AI: **Google Gemini** (free key from Google AI Studio, `gemini-2.0-flash`), structured JSON output,
  with a **mock fallback** so the app fully works before a key is added.
- Streak rule: a day counts if the user **logs ≥1 meal**.
- Goals: **sensible defaults + editable Profile** (no full onboarding flow).
- Camera on web = **file upload / image picker**.

## Tech stack & dependencies
- `expo` (latest SDK via `create-expo-app`), `expo-router` — file-based navigation, web support.
- `react-native-svg` — macro rings + weekly bar chart (no heavy chart lib needed).
- `zustand` + `@react-native-async-storage/async-storage` (persist middleware) — state + local persistence (uses `localStorage` on web).
- `expo-image-picker` — pick/capture photo, returns base64 (works on web as file picker).
- `expo-linear-gradient`, `expo-haptics` (no-op on web) — polish.
- `date-fns` — date/streak helpers.
- TypeScript throughout.

## Project structure
```
ai-calorie-tracker/
  app/
    _layout.tsx                # root stack, theme/StatusBar, store hydration gate
    (tabs)/
      _layout.tsx              # tab bar: Diary · Insights · (center) Add · Profile
      index.tsx                # DIARY: calorie ring + macro rings + today's meals + running total
      insights.tsx             # weekly bar chart, 7-day avg, streak badge
      profile.tsx              # editable goals (cal/P/C/F), reset data
    add.tsx                    # modal: pick image → analyze (AI) → editable result → save
  src/
    components/
      CalorieRing.tsx          # big SVG ring: consumed vs goal, "remaining" center label
      MacroRing.tsx            # small SVG ring per macro (protein/carbs/fat) w/ color
      MacroRings.tsx           # row of 3 MacroRing

      WeeklyBarChart.tsx       # 7-day SVG bars vs goal line
      StreakBadge.tsx          # flame + count

    services/

      gemini.ts                # Gemini REST call w/ responseSchema (structured JSON)
      mockAnalyzer.ts          # realistic randomized fallback (named foods + plausible macros)
    store/

    lib/
      nutrition.ts             # dayTotals(), remaining(), macroCalories()
      streak.ts                # currentStreak(entries)
      date.ts                  # todayISO(), last7Days(), formatting
    theme/
```

# Build a Cal AI Clone — AI Calorie Tracker

## Context
The goal is a polished mobile app (run as a web app for demo/screenshots) where a user
photographs a meal and instantly gets an AI nutritional breakdown — calories + protein/carbs/fat —
plus a daily food diary, macro progress rings, weekly history charts, and streak/goal tracking.
This is a 3-day challenge judged primarily on **screenshots → Loom demo → AI logs → code quality → reflection**,
so the bar is: visually striking, runs end-to-end with real AI, clean architecture.

The directory is empty (no starter template). We build fresh.

**Locked decisions (from the user):**
- Stack: React Native + **Expo (Expo Router)**, run target = **web browser** (`expo start --web`).
- AI: **Google Gemini** (free key from Google AI Studio, `gemini-2.0-flash`), structured JSON output,
  with a **mock fallback** so the app fully works before a key is added.
- Streak rule: a day counts if the user **logs ≥1 meal**.
- Goals: **sensible defaults + editable Profile** (no full onboarding flow).
- Camera on web = **file upload / image picker**.

## Submission requirements (set up FIRST, before any code)
The challenge **rejects submissions without an `/ai-logs/` folder**, and logs can't be reconstructed
retroactively — so this is execution step 0:
- `git init` the project; create a **public** `github.com` repo and push (ZIP uploads not accepted).
- Create `./ai-logs/` at repo root immediately and commit it (with a short README explaining it holds
  the Claude Code build conversation).
- Claude Code logs for this project live at `~/.claude/projects/-Users-honey-AI-Calorie-Tracker/`
  (`.md` / `.jsonl`). Copy them into `./ai-logs/` and commit **repeatedly as we build** (not just at the end).
- Add a tiny `scripts/sync-ai-logs.sh` that `cp`s the latest logs into `./ai-logs/` so syncing is one command.
- `.gitignore` must include `node_modules/`, `.env` (real key never committed) — but keep `.env.example` and `ai-logs/`.
- Final submission package = public GitHub URL (with `/ai-logs/`) + Loom walkthrough + up to 15 screenshots + written reflection.

## Tech stack & dependencies
- `expo` (latest SDK via `create-expo-app`), `expo-router` — file-based navigation, web support.
- `react-native-svg` — macro rings + weekly bar chart (no heavy chart lib needed).
- `zustand` + `@react-native-async-storage/async-storage` (persist middleware) — state + local persistence (uses `localStorage` on web).
- `expo-image-picker` — pick/capture photo, returns base64 (works on web as file picker).
- `expo-linear-gradient`, `expo-haptics` (no-op on web) — polish.
- `date-fns` — date/streak helpers.
- TypeScript throughout.

## Project structure
```
ai-calorie-tracker/
  app/
    _layout.tsx                # root stack, theme/StatusBar, store hydration gate
    (tabs)/
      _layout.tsx              # tab bar: Diary · Insights · (center) Add · Profile
      index.tsx                # DIARY: calorie ring + macro rings + today's meals + running total
      insights.tsx             # weekly bar chart, 7-day avg, streak badge
      profile.tsx              # editable goals (cal/P/C/F), reset data
    add.tsx                    # modal: pick image → analyze (AI) → editable result → save
  src/
    components/
      CalorieRing.tsx          # big SVG ring: consumed vs goal, "remaining" center label
      MacroRing.tsx            # small SVG ring per macro (protein/carbs/fat) w/ color
      MacroRings.tsx           # row of 3 MacroRing
      MealCard.tsx             # thumbnail, name, kcal, macro chips, swipe/long-press delete
      WeeklyBarChart.tsx       # 7-day SVG bars vs goal line
      StreakBadge.tsx          # flame + count
      EmptyState.tsx
      Screen.tsx               # safe-area + bg wrapper
    services/
      ai.ts                    # analyzeFoodImage(base64) -> Nutrition  (chooses gemini | mock)
      gemini.ts                # Gemini REST call w/ responseSchema (structured JSON)
      mockAnalyzer.ts          # realistic randomized fallback (named foods + plausible macros)
    store/
      useDiaryStore.ts         # zustand: entries[], goals, add/remove/update, selectors
    lib/
      nutrition.ts             # dayTotals(), remaining(), macroCalories()
      streak.ts                # currentStreak(entries)
      date.ts                  # todayISO(), last7Days(), formatting
    theme/
      colors.ts, typography.ts # dark, vibrant palette; macro colors
  assets/                      # icon, splash, sample food images for demo
  .env.example                 # EXPO_PUBLIC_GEMINI_API_KEY=
  app.json
```

## Data model (TypeScript)
```ts
type Nutrition = { name: string; servingDesc: string;
                   calories: number; protein: number; carbs: number; fat: number;
                   confidence?: number };
type FoodEntry = Nutrition & { id: string; dateISO: string; createdAt: number;
                               imageUri?: string; source: 'ai' | 'manual' };
type Goals = { calories: number; protein: number; carbs: number; fat: number };
// store: { entries: FoodEntry[]; goals: Goals (default 2000 / 150P / 200C / 65F) }
```

## Implementation steps
0. **Repo + AI logs (do first)**: `git init`, create public GitHub repo, add `./ai-logs/` + `scripts/sync-ai-logs.sh` + `.gitignore`, copy current Claude logs in, commit & push. Re-sync/commit logs periodically through the build.
1. **Scaffold**: `npx create-expo-app@latest ai-calorie-tracker -t` (Expo Router + TS template) inside the working dir; install deps above; add `.env.example` and `theme/`.
2. **Store** (`useDiaryStore.ts`): zustand + persist(AsyncStorage). Actions: `addEntry`, `removeEntry`, `updateEntry`, `setGoals`. Selectors: `entriesForDate`, `dayTotals`. Add a hydration flag so the root layout can gate render until storage loads.
3. **AI service**:
   - `gemini.ts`: POST to `generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/models/gemini-2.0-flash:generateContent?key=…` with the base64 image part + a prompt; use `responseMimeType: application/json` + `responseSchema` to force the `Nutrition` shape. Parse + clamp/sanitize numbers.
   - `ai.ts`: if `EXPO_PUBLIC_GEMINI_API_KEY` present → gemini, else → `mockAnalyzer` (and fall back to mock on any network/parse error so the demo never breaks). Note in code the key-exposure tradeoff (client-side key is fine for a demo; a thin proxy would be the production fix).
4. **Add flow** (`add.tsx`): `expo-image-picker` → show image → "Analyzing…" state → call `ai.analyzeFoodImage` → editable form (name, serving, kcal, P/C/F sliders/inputs, confidence pill) → Save → `addEntry` → route back to Diary. Manual-add path (skip photo) for completeness.
5. **Diary** (`index.tsx`): header date; `CalorieRing` (consumed/goal, remaining in center); `MacroRings` row; running total; list of today's `MealCard`s with delete; FAB/empty state linking to Add.
6. **Insights** (`insights.tsx`): `WeeklyBarChart` (last 7 days calories with goal line), 7-day average, `StreakBadge` via `lib/streak.ts` (consecutive days ending today with ≥1 entry).
7. **Profile** (`profile.tsx`): edit goal numbers (with live macro-calorie sanity), reset data, small about/AI-status line (shows whether real AI or mock is active).
8. **Polish**: consistent dark theme, rounded cards, gradient accents, ring fill animation (Animated/Reanimated), haptics on save, seed a few sample entries on first run so screenshots look full, app icon/splash.

## Key reuse / conventions
- One SVG ring primitive (`MacroRing`) parameterized by color/size; `CalorieRing` reuses the same arc math from `lib/nutrition.ts`.
- All totals/streak logic lives in `lib/` (pure functions) — easy to read and unit-friendly.
- Single source of AI truth in `services/ai.ts`; screens never call Gemini directly.
- Colors centralized in `theme/colors.ts` (protein/carbs/fat each a fixed hue used everywhere).

## Verification (end-to-end)
1. `cd ai-calorie-tracker && npx expo start --web` → opens in browser.
2. Without a key: Add → pick a food image → mock analysis returns plausible macros → save → Diary rings + running total update; add 2–3 → list grows.
3. Insights tab: weekly bars render, streak shows ≥1; Profile: change calorie goal → ring re-scales.
4. With a key: put `EXPO_PUBLIC_GEMINI_API_KEY=…` in `.env`, restart, upload a real food photo → verify Gemini returns real recognition + macros; kill network → confirm graceful mock fallback.
5. Sanity: delete an entry → totals decrease; reload page → data persists (AsyncStorage/localStorage).

## Deliverables for the challenge (after build)
- Screenshots of all flows (Diary/rings, Add/AI result, Insights/charts, Profile).
- README with setup + how to get a free Gemini key + the AI-integration writeup.
- (User records the Loom + reflection.)

## Notes / tradeoffs
- Client-side Gemini key is acceptable for this demo; production would proxy via a server.
- Web target means `expo-camera` live capture is replaced by image upload — intentional for screenshot quality. The same code runs on a device via Expo Go if desired.

# Build a Cal AI Clone — AI Calorie Tracker

## Context
The goal is a polished mobile app (run as a web app for demo/screenshots) where a user
photographs a meal and instantly gets an AI nutritional breakdown — calories + protein/carbs/fat —
plus a daily food diary, macro progress rings, weekly history charts, and streak/goal tracking.
This is a 3-day challenge judged primarily on **screenshots → Loom demo → AI logs → code quality → reflection**,
so the bar is: visually striking, runs end-to-end with real AI, clean architecture.

The directory is empty (no starter template). We build fresh.

**Locked decisions (from the user):**
- Stack: React Native + **Expo (Expo Router)**, run target = **web browser** (`expo start --web`).
- AI: **Google Gemini** (free key from Google AI Studio, `gemini-2.0-flash`), structured JSON output,
  with a **mock fallback** so the app fully works before a key is added.
- Streak rule: a day counts if the user **logs ≥1 meal**.
- Goals: **sensible defaults + editable Profile** (no full onboarding flow).
- Camera on web = **file upload / image picker**.

## Submission requirements (set up FIRST, before any code)
The challenge **rejects submissions without an `/ai-logs/` folder**, and logs can't be reconstructed
retroactively — so this is execution step 0:
- `git init` the project; create a **public** `github.com` repo and push (ZIP uploads not accepted).
  - ⚠️ `gh` CLI is not installed and git identity is unset on this machine. Two options:
    (a) install + auth `gh` (`brew install gh && gh auth login`) so I can create/push the repo, or
    (b) you create an empty public repo on github.com and paste the URL — I'll set git identity, add the remote, and push.
    I'll confirm which you prefer at execution time.
- Create `./ai-logs/` at repo root immediately and commit it (with a short README explaining it holds
  the Claude Code build conversation).
- Claude Code logs for this project live at `~/.claude/projects/-Users-honey-AI-Calorie-Tracker/`
  (`.md` / `.jsonl`). Copy them into `./ai-logs/` and commit **repeatedly as we build** (not just at the end).
- Add a tiny `scripts/sync-ai-logs.sh` that `cp`s the latest logs into `./ai-logs/` so syncing is one command.
- `.gitignore` must include `node_modules/`, `.env` (real key never committed) — but keep `.env.example` and `ai-logs/`.
- Final submission package = public GitHub URL (with `/ai-logs/`) + Loom walkthrough + up to 15 screenshots + written reflection.

## Tech stack & dependencies
- `expo` (latest SDK via `create-expo-app`), `expo-router` — file-based navigation, web support.
- `react-native-svg` — macro rings + weekly bar chart (no heavy chart lib needed).
- `zustand` + `@react-native-async-storage/async-storage` (persist middleware) — state + local persistence (uses `localStorage` on web).
- `expo-image-picker` — pick/capture photo, returns base64 (works on web as file picker).
- `expo-linear-gradient`, `expo-haptics` (no-op on web) — polish.
- `date-fns` — date/streak helpers.
- TypeScript throughout.

## Project structure
```
ai-calorie-tracker/
  app/
    _layout.tsx                # root stack, theme/StatusBar, store hydration gate
    (tabs)/
      _layout.tsx              # tab bar: Diary · Insights · (center) Add · Profile
      index.tsx                # DIARY: calorie ring + macro rings + today's meals + running total
      insights.tsx             # weekly bar chart, 7-day avg, streak badge
      profile.tsx              # editable goals (cal/P/C/F), reset data
    add.tsx                    # modal: pick image → analyze (AI) → editable result → save
  src/
    components/
      CalorieRing.tsx          # big SVG ring: consumed vs goal, "remaining" center label
      MacroRing.tsx            # small SVG ring per macro (protein/carbs/fat) w/ color
      MacroRings.tsx           # row of 3 MacroRing
      MealCard.tsx             # thumbnail, name, kcal, macro chips, swipe/long-press delete
      WeeklyBarChart.tsx       # 7-day SVG bars vs goal line
      StreakBadge.tsx          # flame + count
      EmptyState.tsx
      Screen.tsx               # safe-area + bg wrapper
    services/
      ai.ts                    # analyzeFoodImage(base64) -> Nutrition  (chooses gemini | mock)
      gemini.ts                # Gemini REST call w/ responseSchema (structured JSON)
      mockAnalyzer.ts          # realistic randomized fallback (named foods + plausible macros)
    store/
      useDiaryStore.ts         # zustand: entries[], goals, add/remove/update, selectors
    lib/
      nutrition.ts             # dayTotals(), remaining(), macroCalories()
      streak.ts                # currentStreak(entries)
      date.ts                  # todayISO(), last7Days(), formatting
    theme/
      colors.ts, typography.ts # dark, vibrant palette; macro colors
  assets/                      # icon, splash, sample food images for demo
  .env.example                 # EXPO_PUBLIC_GEMINI_API_KEY=
  app.json
```

## Data model (TypeScript)
```ts
type Nutrition = { name: string; servingDesc: string;
                   calories: number; protein: number; carbs: number; fat: number;
                   confidence?: number };
type FoodEntry = Nutrition & { id: string; dateISO: string; createdAt: number;
                               imageUri?: string; source: 'ai' | 'manual' };
type Goals = { calories: number; protein: number; carbs: number; fat: number };
// store: { entries: FoodEntry[]; goals: Goals (default 2000 / 150P / 200C / 65F) }
```

## Implementation steps
0. **Repo + AI logs (do first)**: `git init`, create public GitHub repo, add `./ai-logs/` + `scripts/sync-ai-logs.sh` + `.gitignore`, copy current Claude logs in, commit & push. Re-sync/commit logs periodically through the build.
1. **Scaffold**: `npx create-expo-app@latest ai-calorie-tracker -t` (Expo Router + TS template) inside the working dir; install deps above; add `.env.example` and `theme/`.
2. **Store** (`useDiaryStore.ts`): zustand + persist(AsyncStorage). Actions: `addEntry`, `removeEntry`, `updateEntry`, `setGoals`. Selectors: `entriesForDate`, `dayTotals`. Add a hydration flag so the root layout can gate render until storage loads.
3. **AI service**:
   - `gemini.ts`: POST to `generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/models/gemini-2.0-flash:generateContent?key=…` with the base64 image part + a prompt; use `responseMimeType: application/json` + `responseSchema` to force the `Nutrition` shape. Parse + clamp/sanitize numbers.
   - `ai.ts`: if `EXPO_PUBLIC_GEMINI_API_KEY` present → gemini, else → `mockAnalyzer` (and fall back to mock on any network/parse error so the demo never breaks). Note in code the key-exposure tradeoff (client-side key is fine for a demo; a thin proxy would be the production fix).
4. **Add flow** (`add.tsx`): `expo-image-picker` → show image → "Analyzing…" state → call `ai.analyzeFoodImage` → editable form (name, serving, kcal, P/C/F sliders/inputs, confidence pill) → Save → `addEntry` → route back to Diary. Manual-add path (skip photo) for completeness.
5. **Diary** (`index.tsx`): header date; `CalorieRing` (consumed/goal, remaining in center); `MacroRings` row; running total; list of today's `MealCard`s with delete; FAB/empty state linking to Add.
6. **Insights** (`insights.tsx`): `WeeklyBarChart` (last 7 days calories with goal line), 7-day average, `StreakBadge` via `lib/streak.ts` (consecutive days ending today with ≥1 entry).
7. **Profile** (`profile.tsx`): edit goal numbers (with live macro-calorie sanity), reset data, small about/AI-status line (shows whether real AI or mock is active).
8. **Polish**: consistent dark theme, rounded cards, gradient accents, ring fill animation (Animated/Reanimated), haptics on save, seed a few sample entries on first run so screenshots look full, app icon/splash.

## Key reuse / conventions
- One SVG ring primitive (`MacroRing`) parameterized by color/size; `CalorieRing` reuses the same arc math from `lib/nutrition.ts`.
- All totals/streak logic lives in `lib/` (pure functions) — easy to read and unit-friendly.
- Single source of AI truth in `services/ai.ts`; screens never call Gemini directly.
- Colors centralized in `theme/colors.ts` (protein/carbs/fat each a fixed hue used everywhere).

## Verification (end-to-end)
1. `cd ai-calorie-tracker && npx expo start --web` → opens in browser.
2. Without a key: Add → pick a food image → mock analysis returns plausible macros → save → Diary rings + running total update; add 2–3 → list grows.
3. Insights tab: weekly bars render, streak shows ≥1; Profile: change calorie goal → ring re-scales.
4. With a key: put `EXPO_PUBLIC_GEMINI_API_KEY=…` in `.env`, restart, upload a real food photo → verify Gemini returns real recognition + macros; kill network → confirm graceful mock fallback.
5. Sanity: delete an entry → totals decrease; reload page → data persists (AsyncStorage/localStorage).

## Deliverables for the challenge (after build)
- **Public GitHub repo** with `/ai-logs/` populated (committed throughout — required, or submission is rejected).
- Up to 15 **screenshots** of all flows (Diary/rings, Add/AI result, Insights/charts, Profile).
- **README** with setup + how to get a free Gemini key + the AI-integration writeup.
- (User records the **Loom** walkthrough + writes the **reflection**; I'll draft a reflection template in the README.)

## Notes / tradeoffs
- Client-side Gemini key is acceptable for this demo; production would proxy via a server.
- Web target means `expo-camera` live capture is replaced by image upload — intentional for screenshot quality. The same code runs on a device via Expo Go if desired.

```
ai-calorie-tracker/

```

## Implementation steps
0. **Repo + AI logs (do first)**: `git init`, create public GitHub repo, add `./ai-logs/` + `scripts/sync-ai-logs.sh` + `.gitignore`, copy current Claude logs in, commit & push. Re-sync/commit logs periodically through the build.
1. **Scaffold**: `npx create-expo-app@latest ai-calorie-tracker -t` (Expo Router + TS template) inside the working dir; install deps above; add `.env.example` and `theme/`.
2. **Store** (`useDiaryStore.ts`): zustand + persist(AsyncStorage). Actions: `addEntry`, `removeEntry`, `updateEntry`, `setGoals`. Selectors: `entriesForDate`, `dayTotals`. Add a hydration flag so the root layout can gate render until storage loads.
3. **AI service**:
   - `gemini.ts`: POST to `generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/models/gemini-2.0-flash:generateContent?key=…` with the base64 image part + a prompt; use `responseMimeType: application/json` + `responseSchema` to force the `Nutrition` shape. Parse + clamp/sanitize numbers.
   - `ai.ts`: if `EXPO_PUBLIC_GEMINI_API_KEY` present → gemini, else → `mockAnalyzer` (and fall back to mock on any network/parse error so the demo never breaks). Note in code the key-exposure tradeoff (client-side key is fine for a demo; a thin proxy would be the production fix).
4. **Add flow** (`add.tsx`): `expo-image-picker` → show image → "Analyzing…" state → call `ai.analyzeFoodImage` → editable form (name, serving, kcal, P/C/F sliders/inputs, confidence pill) → Save → `addEntry` → route back to Diary. Manual-add path (skip photo) for completeness.
5. **Diary** (`index.tsx`): header date; `CalorieRing` (consumed/goal, remaining in center); `MacroRings` row; running total; list of today's `MealCard`s with delete; FAB/empty state linking to Add.
6. **Insights** (`insights.tsx`): `WeeklyBarChart` (last 7 days calories with goal line), 7-day average, `StreakBadge` via `lib/streak.ts` (consecutive days ending today with ≥1 entry).
7. **Profile** (`profile.tsx`): edit goal numbers (with live macro-calorie sanity), reset data, small about/AI-status line (shows whether real AI or mock is active).
8. **Polish**: consistent dark theme, rounded cards, gradient accents, ring fill animation (Animated/Reanimated), haptics on save, seed a few sample entries on first run so screenshots look full, app icon/splash.

## Key reuse / conventions
- One SVG ring primitive (`MacroRing`) parameterized by color/size; `CalorieRing` reuses the same arc math from `lib/nutrition.ts`.
- All totals/streak logic lives in `lib/` (pure functions) — easy to read and unit-friendly.
- Single source of AI truth in `services/ai.ts`; screens never call Gemini directly.
- Colors centralized in `theme/colors.ts` (protein/carbs/fat each a fixed hue used everywhere).

## Verification (end-to-end)
1. `cd ai-calorie-tracker && npx expo start --web` → opens in browser.
2. Without a key: Add → pick a food image → mock analysis returns plausible macros → save → Diary rings + running total update; add 2–3 → list grows.
3. Insights tab: weekly bars render, streak shows ≥1; Profile: change calorie goal → ring re-scales.
4. With a key: put `EXPO_PUBLIC_GEMINI_API_KEY=…` in `.env`, restart, upload a real food photo → verify Gemini returns real recognition + macros; kill network → confirm graceful mock fallback.
5. Sanity: delete an entry → totals decrease; reload page → data persists (AsyncStorage/localStorage).

## Deliverables for the challenge (after build)
- **Public GitHub repo** with `/ai-logs/` populated (committed throughout — required, or submission is rejected).
- Up to 15 **screenshots** of all flows (Diary/rings, Add/AI result, Insights/charts, Profile).
- **README** with setup + how to get a free Gemini key + the AI-integration writeup.
- (User records the **Loom** walkthrough + writes the **reflection**; I'll draft a reflection template in the README.)

## Notes / tradeoffs
- Client-side Gemini key is acceptable for this demo; production would proxy via a server.
- Web target means `expo-camera` live capture is replaced by image upload — intentional for screenshot quality. The same code runs on a device via Expo Go if desired.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Copies the Claude Code conversation logs for this project into ./ai-logs/
# so they can be committed for the challenge submission.
# Usage: bash scripts/sync-ai-logs.sh
set -euo pipefail

SRC="$HOME/.claude/projects/-Users-honey-AI-Calorie-Tracker"
DEST="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)/ai-logs"

mkdir -p "$DEST"

if [ -d "$SRC" ]; then
  # Copy conversation transcripts (.jsonl) and any rendered markdown (.md)
  find "$SRC" -maxdepth 1 -type f \( -name '*.jsonl' -o -name '*.md' \) -exec cp {} "$DEST/" \;
  echo "Synced AI logs from: $SRC"
  ls -1 "$DEST"
else
  echo "No Claude logs found at $SRC" >&2
  exit 1
fi

input.content:
# 🥗 NutriSnap — AI Calorie Tracker

Snap a photo of any meal and instantly get calories, macros, and a full nutritional
breakdown powered by AI. NutriSnap is a Cal AI–style calorie tracker built with
**React Native + Expo**, with a polished daily diary, macro progress rings, weekly
history charts, and streak tracking.

> Built for the *Cal AI Clone* challenge. Runs as a web app (and on iOS/Android via Expo).

## ✨ Features

- 📷 **AI food recognition** — photograph or upload a meal; Google **Gemini Vision** identifies the dish and estimates calories + protein/carbs/fat.
- ✍️ **Review & edit** — every AI result is fully editable before saving (name, serving, calories, macros) with an AI-confidence indicator.
- 📔 **Daily diary** — running calorie total with a hero **calorie ring** and per-meal cards.
- 🎯 **Macro progress rings** — protein, carbs and fat each get an animated SVG ring vs. your goal.
- 📊 **Weekly insights** — 7-day calorie bar chart with a goal line, daily average, days-on-track, and average macros.
- 🔥 **Streak tracking** — consecutive days with a logged meal, plus your best streak.
- ⚙️ **Editable goals** — set calorie & macro targets on the Profile tab (sensible defaults out of the box).
- 💾 **Offline-first** — everything persists locally (AsyncStorage / localStorage). Works with **zero backend**.
- 🧪 **Graceful fallback** — no API key? The app runs in demo mode with realistic mock results, so it never breaks.

## 📸 Screenshots

| Diary | Insights | Add (AI) | Review | Profile |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| ![Diary](docs/screenshots/01-diary.png) | ![Insights](docs/screenshots/02-insights.png) | ![Add](docs/screenshots/03-add.png) | ![Review](docs/screenshots/04-review.png) | ![Profile](docs/screenshots/05-profile.png) |

## 🚀 Getting started

```bash
npm install
npm run web        # opens the app in your browser (or: npx expo start)
```

Then press `w` for web, `i` for iOS simulator, or scan the QR code with **Expo Go**.

### Enable real AI (free)

The app works immediately in **demo mode**. To turn on live recognition:

1. Get a **free** Gemini API key at <https://aistudio.google.com/apikey>.
2. Copy `.env.example` → `.env` and paste your key:
   ```
   EXPO_PUBLIC_GEMINI_API_KEY=your_key_here
   ```
3. Restart `npx expo start`. The Profile tab shows **“Gemini Vision (live)”** when active.

## 🧠 AI integration

Food recognition lives behind a single service (`src/services/ai.ts`) so the UI never
talks to a provider directly:

- **`gemini.ts`** sends the base64 image + a nutrition prompt to `gemini-2.0-flash`, using
  Gemini's **structured output** (`responseMimeType: application/json` + `responseSchema`)
  to force a typed `{ name, servingDesc, calories, protein, carbs, fat, confidence }` result.
- Responses are **sanitized**: numbers are clamped to sane ranges, and if the model's
  calorie figure disagrees badly with its own macros, we recompute from macros (4/4/9 kcal/g).
- **`ai.ts`** decides Gemini vs. mock based on whether a key is set, and **falls back to mock
  on any network/parse error** — the demo never dead-ends.

> **Security note:** the key is read from `EXPO_PUBLIC_GEMINI_API_KEY` and the call is made
> client-side. That's fine for a demo; the production fix is a thin server proxy that holds
> the key. This is documented in `gemini.ts`.

## 🏗️ Architecture

```
src/
  app/                      # Expo Router (file-based routing)
    _layout.tsx             # root stack (tabs + add modal), dark theme
    (tabs)/
      _layout.tsx           # bottom tabs + center "scan" FAB
      index.tsx             # Diary: calorie ring, macro rings, today's meals
      insights.tsx          # weekly chart, stats, streak
      profile.tsx           # editable goals + AI status
    add.tsx                 # pick → analyze → review → save (modal)
  components/                # CalorieRing, MacroRings, ProgressRing, MealCard,
                             # WeeklyBarChart, StreakBadge, Card, Screen, EmptyState
  services/                  # ai.ts (entry), gemini.ts, mockAnalyzer.ts
  store/                     # useDiaryStore.ts (zustand + persist), seed.ts
  lib/                       # nutrition.ts, streak.ts, date.ts, types.ts (pure logic)
  theme/                     # colors, spacing, radius, fonts
```

Principles: **pure logic in `lib/`** (totals, streaks, dates — easy to read/test),
a **single AI source of truth** in `services/`, and **one reusable SVG ring** primitive
that powers both the calorie ring and the three macro rings.

## 🛠️ Tech stack

React Native · Expo (SDK 56) · Expo Router · TypeScript · Zustand · AsyncStorage ·
react-native-svg · expo-image-picker · Google Gemini Vision.

## 🤔 Reflection

**What was easy.** Expo + Expo Router made the multi-screen structure quick to stand up,
and Gemini's structured-output mode meant the AI returned clean typed JSON instead of prose
I'd have to parse. Keeping all nutrition math as pure functions made the diary, rings, and
charts share one source of truth.

**What was tricky.** Two web-specific gotchas: (1) Expo's default **static web rendering**
prerenders in Node, where `window`/`localStorage` don't exist — switching `web.output` to
`single` (SPA) fixed it. (2) Animating SVG `strokeDashoffset` via React Native's `Animated`
is unreliable on `react-native-web`, so the progress rings render their arc from real React
state instead. Getting a responsive centered layout right across phone and desktop widths also
took iteration.

**What I'd change with more time.** Add a server proxy for the API key, multi-item detection
per photo, a barcode/manual-search database for packaged foods, and per-meal grouping
(breakfast/lunch/dinner). I'd also add unit tests around `lib/streak.ts` and `lib/nutrition.ts`.

## 📁 AI logs

The `/ai-logs` folder contains the Claude Code conversation used to build this app.
Re-sync with `bash scripts/sync-ai-logs.sh`.

# 🥗 NutriSnap — AI Calorie Tracker

Snap a photo of any meal and instantly get calories, macros, and a full nutritional
breakdown powered by AI. NutriSnap is a Cal AI–style calorie tracker built with
**React Native + Expo**, with a polished daily diary, macro progress rings, weekly
history charts, and streak tracking.

> Built for the *Cal AI Clone* challenge. Runs as a web app (and on iOS/Android via Expo).

## ✨ Features

- 📷 **AI food recognition** — photograph or upload a meal; Google **Gemini Vision** identifies the dish and estimates calories + protein/carbs/fat.
- ✍️ **Review & edit** — every AI result is fully editable before saving (name, serving, calories, macros) with an AI-confidence indicator.
- 📔 **Daily diary** — running calorie total with a hero **calorie ring** and per-meal cards.
- 🎯 **Macro progress rings** — protein, carbs and fat each get an SVG ring vs. your goal.
- 📊 **Weekly insights** — 7-day calorie bar chart with a goal line, daily average, days-on-track, and average macros.
- 🔥 **Streak tracking** — consecutive days with a logged meal, plus your best streak.
- ⚙️ **Editable goals** — set calorie & macro targets on the Profile tab (sensible defaults out of the box).
- 💾 **Offline-first** — everything persists locally (AsyncStorage / localStorage). Works with **zero backend**.
- 🧪 **Graceful fallback** — no API key? The app runs in demo mode with realistic mock results, so it never breaks.

## 📸 Screenshots

| Diary | Insights | Add (AI) | Review | Profile |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| ![Diary](docs/screenshots/01-diary.png) | ![Insights](docs/screenshots/02-insights.png) | ![Add](docs/screenshots/03-add.png) | ![Review](docs/screenshots/04-review.png) | ![Profile](docs/screenshots/05-profile.png) |

## 🚀 Getting started

```bash
npm install
npm run web        # opens the app in your browser (or: npx expo start)
```

Then press `w` for web, `i` for iOS simulator, or scan the QR code with **Expo Go**.

### Enable real AI (free)

The app works immediately in **demo mode**. To turn on live recognition:

1. Get a **free** Gemini API key at <https://aistudio.google.com/apikey>.
2. Copy `.env.example` → `.env` and paste your key:
   ```
   EXPO_PUBLIC_GEMINI_API_KEY=your_key_here
   ```
3. Restart `npx expo start`. The Profile tab shows **“Gemini Vision (live)”** when active.

## 🧠 AI integration

Food recognition lives behind a single service (`src/services/ai.ts`) so the UI never
talks to a provider directly:

- **`gemini.ts`** sends the base64 image + a nutrition prompt to `gemini-2.0-flash`, using
  Gemini's **structured output** (`responseMimeType: application/json` + `responseSchema`)
  to force a typed `{ name, servingDesc, calories, protein, carbs, fat, confidence }` result.
- Responses are **sanitized**: numbers are clamped to sane ranges, and if the model's
  calorie figure disagrees badly with its own macros, we recompute from macros (4/4/9 kcal/g).
- **`ai.ts`** decides Gemini vs. mock based on whether a key is set, and **falls back to mock
  on any network/parse error** — the demo never dead-ends.

> **Security note:** the key is read from `EXPO_PUBLIC_GEMINI_API_KEY` and the call is made
> client-side. That's fine for a demo; the production fix is a thin server proxy that holds
> the key. This is documented in `gemini.ts`.

## 🏗️ Architecture

```
src/
  app/                      # Expo Router (file-based routing)
    _layout.tsx             # root stack (tabs + add modal), dark theme
    (tabs)/
      _layout.tsx           # bottom tabs + center "scan" FAB
      index.tsx             # Diary: calorie ring, macro rings, today's meals
      insights.tsx          # weekly chart, stats, streak
      profile.tsx           # editable goals + AI status
    add.tsx                 # pick → analyze → review → save (modal)
  components/                # CalorieRing, MacroRings, ProgressRing, MealCard,
                             # WeeklyBarChart, StreakBadge, Card, Screen, EmptyState
  services/                  # ai.ts (entry), gemini.ts, mockAnalyzer.ts
  store/                     # useDiaryStore.ts (zustand + persist), seed.ts
  lib/                       # nutrition.ts, streak.ts, date.ts, types.ts (pure logic)
  theme/                     # colors, spacing, radius, fonts
```

Principles: **pure logic in `lib/`** (totals, streaks, dates — easy to read/test),
a **single AI source of truth** in `services/`, and **one reusable SVG ring** primitive
that powers both the calorie ring and the three macro rings.

## 🛠️ Tech stack

React Native · Expo (SDK 56) · Expo Router · TypeScript · Zustand · AsyncStorage ·
react-native-svg · expo-image-picker · Google Gemini Vision.

## 🤔 Reflection

**What was easy.** Expo + Expo Router made the multi-screen structure quick to stand up,
and Gemini's structured-output mode meant the AI returned clean typed JSON instead of prose
to parse. Keeping all nutrition math as pure functions let the diary, rings, and charts share
one source of truth.

**What was tricky.** Two web-specific gotchas: (1) Expo's default **static web rendering**
prerenders in Node, where `window`/`localStorage` don't exist — switching `web.output` to
`single` (SPA) fixed it. (2) Animating SVG `strokeDashoffset` via React Native's `Animated`
is unreliable on `react-native-web`, so the progress rings render their arc from real React
state instead. Getting a responsive, centered layout right across phone and desktop widths
also took iteration.

**What I'd change with more time.** Add a server proxy for the API key, multi-item detection
per photo, a barcode / manual-search database for packaged foods, and per-meal grouping
(breakfast / lunch / dinner). I'd also add unit tests around `lib/streak.ts` and `lib/nutrition.ts`.

## 📁 AI logs

The `/ai-logs` folder contains the Claude Code conversation used to build this app.
Re-sync with `bash scripts/sync-ai-logs.sh`.

# Welcome to your Expo app 👋

This is an [Expo](https://expo.dev) project created with [`create-expo-app`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/create-expo-app).

## Get started

1. Install dependencies

   ```bash
   npm install
   ```

2. Start the app

   ```bash
   npx expo start
   ```

In the output, you'll find options to open the app in a

- [development build](https://docs.expo.dev/develop/development-builds/introduction/)
- [Android emulator](https://docs.expo.dev/workflow/android-studio-emulator/)
- [iOS simulator](https://docs.expo.dev/workflow/ios-simulator/)
- [Expo Go](https://expo.dev/go), a limited sandbox for trying out app development with Expo

You can start developing by editing the files inside the **app** directory. This project uses [file-based routing](https://docs.expo.dev/router/introduction).

## Get a fresh project

When you're ready, run:

```bash
npm run reset-project
```

This command will move the starter code to the **app-example** directory and create a blank **app** directory where you can start developing.

### Other setup steps

- To set up ESLint for linting, run `npx expo lint`, or follow our guide on ["Using ESLint and Prettier"](https://docs.expo.dev/guides/using-eslint/)
- If you'd like to set up unit testing, follow our guide on ["Unit Testing with Jest"](https://docs.expo.dev/develop/unit-testing/)
- Learn more about the TypeScript setup in this template in our guide on ["Using TypeScript"](https://docs.expo.dev/guides/typescript/)

## Learn more

To learn more about developing your project with Expo, look at the following resources:

- [Expo documentation](https://docs.expo.dev/): Learn fundamentals, or go into advanced topics with our [guides](https://docs.expo.dev/guides).
- [Learn Expo tutorial](https://docs.expo.dev/tutorial/introduction/): Follow a step-by-step tutorial where you'll create a project that runs on Android, iOS, and the web.

## Join the community

Join our community of developers creating universal apps.

- [Expo on GitHub](https://github.com/expo/expo): View our open source platform and contribute.
- [Discord community](https://chat.expo.dev): Chat with Expo users and ask questions.

A complete Cal AI–style app, verified rendering end-to-end on web. Everything is committed locally (`01a7d2e`) and ready for you to push.

### To publish + run


#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Copies the Claude Code conversation logs for this project into ./ai-logs/
# so they can be committed for the challenge submission.
# Usage: bash scripts/sync-ai-logs.sh
set -euo pipefail

SRC="$HOME/.claude/projects/-Users-honey-AI-Calorie-Tracker"
DEST="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)/ai-logs"

mkdir -p "$DEST"

if [ -d "$SRC" ]; then
  # Copy conversation transcripts (.jsonl) and any rendered markdown (.md)
  find "$SRC" -maxdepth 1 -type f \( -name '*.jsonl' -o -name '*.md' \) -exec cp {} "$DEST/" \;
  echo "Synced AI logs from: $SRC"
  ls -1 "$DEST"
else
  echo "No Claude logs found at $SRC" >&2
  exit 1
fi
